Operation 'composition_tags_update' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary 'Update COMPOSITION tags' is too generic and doesn't clarify that this is a replacement operation. Description lacks clarity on return value and doesn't emphasize the complete replacement semantics. Added 'Replace or update' to clarify operation type, included return value information, and restructured for better AI agent understanding of when to use this operation versus partial updates.

data.current_description:
Updates the list of all ITEM_TAG resources associated with a given target COMPOSITION version or VERSIONED_COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` and owned by EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The `uid_based_id` can take a form of an OBJECT_VERSION_ID identifier taken from VERSION.uid.value (i.e. a `version_uid`), or a form of a HIER_OBJECT_ID identifier taken from VERSIONED_OBJECT.uid.value (i.e. a `versioned_object_uid`).
The former is used to update the tags of a particular COMPOSITION version (e.g. one identified by `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515::openEHRSys.example.com::1`), whereas the latter (e.g. an identifier like `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515`) is be used to update the tags of the target VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container.
  
Providing an empty list will effectively remove all ITEM_TAG associated with the given target.

More than one ITEM_TAG may be associated with a single target, in which case they are uniquely identified by their `key` and `target_path` pair attributes.
data.summary_suggestion: Replace or update COMPOSITION item tags
data.description_suggestion: Replaces the complete list of ITEM_TAG resources associated with a target COMPOSITION version or VERSIONED_COMPOSITION. Accepts a `uid_based_id` parameter that can be either a version-specific OBJECT_VERSION_ID (e.g., `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515::openEHRSys.example.com::1`) to update tags for a specific version, or a HIER_OBJECT_ID (e.g., `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515`) to update tags for the VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container. Providing an empty tag list removes all associated tags. Multiple tags can target the same resource, uniquely identified by their `key` and `target_path` pair. Returns the updated list of ITEM_TAG resources.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Update COMPOSITION tags' is too generic and doesn't clarify that this is a replacement operation. Description lacks clarity on return value and doesn't emphasize the complete replacement semantics. Added 'Replace or update' to clarify operation type, included return value information, and restructured for better AI agent understanding of when to use this operation versus partial updates.

message: Operation 'composition_tags_delete' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary lacks specificity about the `tag_key` parameter. Description doesn't clarify the return status or confirm successful deletion. Enhanced summary to include 'by key' for clarity, added HTTP status code information, and restructured description for better AI agent understanding of the deletion scope and confirmation.

data.current_description:
Deletes the ITEM_TAG resource(s) identified by `tag_key`, associated with a given target COMPOSITION version or VERSIONED_COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` and owned by EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The `uid_based_id` can take a form of an OBJECT_VERSION_ID identifier taken from VERSION.uid.value (i.e. a `version_uid`), or a form of a HIER_OBJECT_ID identifier taken from VERSIONED_OBJECT.uid.value (i.e. a `versioned_object_uid`).
The former is used to delete the tags a particular (target) version of the COMPOSITION version (e.g. one identified by `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515::openEHRSys.example.com::1`), whereas the latter (e.g. an identifier like `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515`) is be used to delete the tags of the target VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container, which semantically are equivalent to tags targeting the latest version.
data.description_suggestion: Removes ITEM_TAG resource(s) identified by the specified `tag_key` from a target COMPOSITION version or VERSIONED_COMPOSITION. The `uid_based_id` parameter accepts either a version-specific OBJECT_VERSION_ID (e.g., `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515::openEHRSys.example.com::1`) to delete tags from a specific version, or a HIER_OBJECT_ID (e.g., `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515`) to delete tags from the VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container (equivalent to targeting the latest version). Returns 204 No Content on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: Summary lacks specificity about the `tag_key` parameter. Description doesn't clarify the return status or confirm successful deletion. Enhanced summary to include 'by key' for clarity, added HTTP status code information, and restructured description for better AI agent understanding of the deletion scope and confirmation.

Providing an empty list will effectively remove all ITEM_TAG associated with the given target.

More than one ITEM_TAG may be associated with a single target, in which case they are uniquely identified by their `key` and `target_path` pair attributes.

message: Operation 'composition_delete' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks clarity on response status and side effects. Added explicit HTTP response code (204 No Content), clarified that deletion creates a new version (not a true deletion), and noted auditability for AI agents to understand the operation's full impact on the EHR system.

data.current_description:
Deletes the COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` and associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The `uid_based_id` MUST be in a form of an OBJECT_VERSION_ID identifier taken from the last (most recent) VERSION.uid.value, representing the `preceding_version_uid` to be deleted.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes the COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` and associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. The `uid_based_id` MUST be in the form of an OBJECT_VERSION_ID identifier taken from the most recent VERSION.uid.value, representing the `preceding_version_uid` to be deleted. Returns HTTP 204 No Content on successful deletion. This operation creates a new deletion version in the VERSIONED_COMPOSITION and is auditable through the contribution history.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on response status and side effects. Added explicit HTTP response code (204 No Content), clarified that deletion creates a new version (not a true deletion), and noted auditability for AI agents to understand the operation's full impact on the EHR system.

data.current_description: Creates a new directory FOLDER associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.
data.summary_suggestion: Create new EHR directory FOLDER structure
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new directory FOLDER associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. Accepts a FOLDER resource in the request body containing the directory structure, name, and optional metadata. Returns HTTP 201 Created with the created FOLDER resource including its assigned uid and version information. The directory becomes the root organizational structure for the EHR and is versioned for audit purposes.

Enhanced to clarify that a FOLDER schema is required as input, specify HTTP 201 response, and explain that the directory is versioned and serves as the organizational root. data.current_description:
Updates directory FOLDER associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The existing latest `version_uid` of directory FOLDER resource (i.e. the `preceding_version_uid`) must be specified in the `If-Match` header.
data.description_suggestion: Updates the directory FOLDER associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. Requires the existing latest `version_uid` of the directory FOLDER resource (the `preceding_version_uid`) to be specified in the `If-Match` header for optimistic concurrency control. Accepts a FOLDER resource in the request body with updated structure and metadata. Returns HTTP 200 OK with the updated FOLDER resource and new version information. Creates a new version in the directory's version history.
data.reasoning: Description explains the If-Match requirement but lacks clarity on response status, request body expectations, and versioning side effects. Enhanced to specify HTTP 200 response, clarify that FOLDER schema is required in request body, explain optimistic concurrency control purpose, and note that updates create new versions. This provides AI agents with complete understanding of the operation's mechanics and effects.

message: Operation 'versioned_ehr_status_version_get_at_time' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary is vague - 'at time' is unclear without context. data.current_summary: Get versioned EHR_STATUS version at time
data.current_description:
Retrieves a VERSION from the VERSIONED_EHR_STATUS associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

If `version_at_time` is supplied, retrieves the VERSION extant _at specified time_, otherwise retrieves the _latest_ VERSION.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve EHR_STATUS version at specified time
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a specific VERSION of the EHR_STATUS from the VERSIONED_EHR_STATUS associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. If `version_at_time` is supplied, retrieves the VERSION that was current at the specified time; otherwise retrieves the latest VERSION. Returns the VERSION object containing the EHR_STATUS data and version metadata.
data.reasoning: Summary is vague - 'at time' is unclear without context. Description lacks clarity about what is returned. Enhanced summary to be more explicit about temporal retrieval. Added output details clarifying that a VERSION object is returned with both status data and metadata, improving AI agent understanding of the response structure.

message: Operation 'composition_create' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate but description lacks clarity about what is returned and the side effects of creation. Added explicit details about the response (created COMPOSITION with version UID), the state change (new VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container created), and the role of ITEM_TAGs. This helps AI agents understand the full lifecycle impact of the operation.

data.current_description:
Creates the first version of a new COMPOSITION in the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

A list of ITEM_TAGs can be associated with the COMPOSITION, in which case a `openehr-item-tag` or `openehr-version-item-tag` request header can be set as well. 
The corresponding response header(s) will return ITEM_TAGs as they were set by the server - see [item tags headers](overview.html#tag/Requests_and_responses/HTTP-headers/openehr-item-tag-and-openehr-version-item-tag) for more details.
data.description_suggestion: Creates the first version of a new COMPOSITION in the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. The request body must contain a valid COMPOSITION resource. Optional ITEM_TAGs can be associated via `openehr-item-tag` or `openehr-version-item-tag` request headers. Returns the created COMPOSITION with its assigned version UID and any server-set ITEM_TAGs in response headers. Creates a new VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container and establishes the initial version in the EHR's composition repository.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description lacks clarity about what is returned and the side effects of creation. Added explicit details about the response (created COMPOSITION with version UID), the state change (new VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container created), and the role of ITEM_TAGs. This helps AI agents understand the full lifecycle impact of the operation.

message: Operation 'composition_get' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary is too generic ('Get COMPOSITION'). Description is verbose and difficult for AI agents to parse due to length and complexity. Improved summary to clarify the dual identifier modes. Restructured description for clarity while preserving essential information about identifier types, temporal retrieval, and response headers. This helps AI agents quickly understand the operation's flexibility and when to use each identifier type.

data.current_description:
Retrieves a version of the COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` and associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The `uid_based_id` can take a form of an OBJECT_VERSION_ID identifier taken from VERSION.uid.value (i.e. a `version_uid`), or a form of a HIER_OBJECT_ID identifier taken from VERSIONED_OBJECT.uid.value (i.e. a `versioned_object_uid`).
The former is used to retrieve a specific known version of the COMPOSITION (e.g. one identified by `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515::openEHRSys.example.com::1`), whereas the latter (e.g. an identifier like `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515`) is be used to retrieve a version from the version container whenever the _version_tree_id_ is unknown or irrelevant (such as when most recent version is requested).

When the `uid_based_id` has the form of a HIER_OBJECT_ID, if the `version_at_time` is supplied, retrieves the version extant _at specified time_, otherwise retrieves the _latest_ COMPOSITION version.

See [Resource identification](overview.html#tag/Resources/Resource-identification) for more details about the identifiers usage and meaning.

When there are ITEM_TAGs associated with the retrieved COMPOSITION, they will be returned as `openehr-item-tag` and `openehr-version-item-tag` response headers, respectively - see [item tags headers](overview.html#tag/Requests_and_responses/HTTP-headers/openehr-item-tag-and-openehr-version-item-tag) for more details.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve COMPOSITION by version or object identifier
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a COMPOSITION version identified by `uid_based_id` from the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. The `uid_based_id` can be either a version-specific OBJECT_VERSION_ID (e.g., `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515::openEHRSys.example.com::1`) to retrieve an exact version, or a HIER_OBJECT_ID (e.g., `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515`) to retrieve from the version container. When using HIER_OBJECT_ID, if `version_at_time` is supplied, retrieves the version current at that time; otherwise retrieves the latest version. Returns the COMPOSITION resource with associated ITEM_TAGs in response headers if present. See Resource identification documentation for identifier format details.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic ('Get COMPOSITION'). Description is verbose and difficult for AI agents to parse due to length and complexity. Improved summary to clarify the dual identifier modes. Restructured description for clarity while preserving essential information about identifier types, temporal retrieval, and response headers. This helps AI agents quickly understand the operation's flexibility and when to use each identifier type.

message: Operation 'composition_update' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate but description lacks clarity about what is returned and the versioning side effects. Added explicit details about the response (updated COMPOSITION with new version UID), the concurrency control mechanism (If-Match header requirement), and the state change (new version created in container). This helps AI agents understand the update semantics and version management implications.

data.current_description:
Updates COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` and associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The `uid_based_id` can take only a form of an HIER_OBJECT_ID identifier taken from VERSIONED_OBJECT.uid.value (i.e. a `versioned_object_uid`).

If the request body already contains a COMPOSITION.uid.value, it must match the `uid_based_id` in the URL. 

The existing latest `version_uid` of COMPOSITION resource (i.e. the `preceding_version_uid`) must be specified in the `If-Match` header.

A list of ITEM_TAGs can be associated with the updated COMPOSITION, in which case a `openehr-item-tag` or `openehr-version-item-tag` request header can be set as well. 
The corresponding response header(s) will return ITEM_TAGs as they were set by the server - see [item tags headers](overview.html#tag/Requests_and_responses/HTTP-headers/openehr-item-tag-and-openehr-version-item-tag) for more details.
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` (HIER_OBJECT_ID form) in the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. The request body must contain the updated COMPOSITION resource; if it includes a uid value, it must match the `uid_based_id` in the URL. The `If-Match` header must contain the current version UID (`preceding_version_uid`) for optimistic concurrency control. Optional ITEM_TAGs can be associated via request headers. Returns the updated COMPOSITION with its new version UID and any server-set ITEM_TAGs in response headers. Creates a new version in the VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container, preserving the version history.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description lacks clarity about what is returned and the versioning side effects. Added explicit details about the response (updated COMPOSITION with new version UID), the concurrency control mechanism (If-Match header requirement), and the state change (new version created in container). This helps AI agents understand the update semantics and version management implications.

message: Operation 'ehr_create' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Create EHR' is too generic and doesn't distinguish this operation from ehr_create_with_id. Description lacks clarity about the return value (newly created EHR with ehr_id) which is critical for AI agents to understand the operation's output. Added specificity about auto-generated identifier type and explicit return value information.

data.current_description:
Create a new `EHR` with an auto-generated identifier.

An EHR_STATUS resource needs to be always created and committed in the new EHR.
This resource MAY be also supplied by the client as the request body. 
If not supplied, a default EHR_STATUS will be used by the service with following attributes:
  - `is_queryable`: true
  - `is_modifiable`: true
  - `subject`: a PARTY_SELF object

All other required EHR attributes and resources will be automatically created as needed by the [EHR creation semantics](https://specifications.openehr.org/releases/RM/latest/ehr.html#_ehr_creation_semantics).
data.summary_suggestion: Create new EHR with auto-generated identifier
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new EHR with an auto-generated HIER_OBJECT_ID identifier. An EHR_STATUS resource is automatically created and committed with the new EHR. The client may optionally supply an EHR_STATUS in the request body; if not provided, a default EHR_STATUS is created with `is_queryable=true`, `is_modifiable=true`, and a PARTY_SELF subject. All other required EHR attributes and resources are automatically initialized per EHR creation semantics. Returns the newly created EHR resource with its auto-generated ehr_id.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Create EHR' is too generic and doesn't distinguish this operation from ehr_create_with_id. Description lacks clarity about the return value (newly created EHR with ehr_id) which is critical for AI agents to understand the operation's output. Added specificity about auto-generated identifier type and explicit return value information.

data.current_description:
Retrieve the EHR with the specified `subject_id` and `subject_namespace`.

These subject parameters will be matched against EHR's EHR_STATUS.subject.external_ref.id.value and 
EHR_STATUS.subject.external_ref.namespace values.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve EHR by subject identifier and namespace
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the EHR associated with the specified subject identifier and namespace. The provided `subject_id` and `subject_namespace` parameters are matched against the EHR_STATUS.subject.external_ref.id.value and EHR_STATUS.subject.external_ref.namespace values respectively. Returns the EHR resource if a matching subject is found. This operation is useful for locating an EHR when the subject's external identifier is known rather than the EHR's internal ehr_id.
data.reasoning: Summary lacks specificity about the matching criteria (namespace). Description is clear about the matching logic but lacks context about when to use this operation versus ehr_get_by_id, and doesn't explicitly state the return value. Added context about use cases and return value for better AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'ehr_create_with_id' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Create EHR with id' is too vague and doesn't clarify that the client specifies the identifier (distinguishing it from ehr_create). Description lacks explicit return value information. Added clarity about client-specified identifier and explicit return value for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Create a new EHR with the specified `ehr_id` identifier.

The value of the `ehr_id` unique identifier MUST be valid [HIER_OBJECT_ID](https://specifications.openehr.org/releases/BASE/latest/base_types.html#_hier_object_id_class) value. 
It is strongly RECOMMENDED that an UUID always be used for this.

An EHR_STATUS resource needs to be always created and committed in the new EHR.
This resource MAY be also supplied by the client as the request body. 
If not supplied, a default EHR_STATUS will be used by the service with following attributes:
  - `is_queryable`: true
  - `is_modifiable`: true
  - `subject`: a PARTY_SELF object

All other required EHR attributes and resources will be automatically created as needed by the [EHR creation semantics](https://specifications.openehr.org/releases/RM/latest/ehr.html#_ehr_creation_semantics).
data.summary_suggestion: Create new EHR with specified identifier
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new EHR with the specified `ehr_id` identifier. The `ehr_id` must be a valid HIER_OBJECT_ID value; UUID format is strongly recommended. An EHR_STATUS resource is automatically created and committed with the new EHR. The client may optionally supply an EHR_STATUS in the request body; if not provided, a default EHR_STATUS is created with `is_queryable=true`, `is_modifiable=true`, and a PARTY_SELF subject. All other required EHR attributes and resources are automatically initialized per EHR creation semantics. Returns the newly created EHR resource with the specified ehr_id.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Create EHR with id' is too vague and doesn't clarify that the client specifies the identifier (distinguishing it from ehr_create). Description lacks explicit return value information. Added clarity about client-specified identifier and explicit return value for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Retrieves a particular version of the EHR_STATUS identified by `version_uid` and associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

When there are ITEM_TAGs associated with the retrieved EHR_STATUS, they will be returned as `openehr-item-tag` and `openehr-version-item-tag` response headers, respectively - see [item tags headers](overview.html#tag/Requests_and_responses/HTTP-headers/openehr-item-tag-and-openehr-version-item-tag) for more details.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a specific version of the EHR_STATUS identified by `version_uid` within the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. Returns the EHR_STATUS resource at the specified version. When ITEM_TAGs are associated with the retrieved EHR_STATUS, they are returned as `openehr-item-tag` and `openehr-version-item-tag` response headers - see item tags headers documentation for details. This operation enables access to historical versions of EHR_STATUS for audit and version tracking purposes.

data.current_description:
Retrieves a version of the EHR_STATUS associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

If `version_at_time` is supplied, retrieves the version extant _at specified time_, otherwise retrieves the _latest_ EHR_STATUS version.

When there are ITEM_TAGs associated with the retrieved EHR_STATUS, they will be returned as `openehr-item-tag` and `openehr-version-item-tag` response headers, respectively - see [item tags headers](overview.html#tag/Requests_and_responses/HTTP-headers/openehr-item-tag-and-openehr-version-item-tag) for more details.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a version of the EHR_STATUS associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. If the `version_at_time` parameter is supplied, retrieves the EHR_STATUS version that was current at the specified time; otherwise retrieves the latest EHR_STATUS version. Returns the EHR_STATUS resource at the requested point in time. When ITEM_TAGs are associated with the retrieved EHR_STATUS, they are returned as `openehr-item-tag` and `openehr-version-item-tag` response headers - see item tags headers documentation for details.

message: Operation 'ehr_status_update' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Summary is adequate but description lacks context about optimistic locking semantics and doesn't clearly explain the conditional return behavior. Added explanation of If-Match header purpose, clarification about version creation, and explicit description of return value conditions to help AI agents understand the operation's side effects and response behavior.

data.current_description:
Updates EHR_STATUS associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The existing latest `version_uid` of EHR_STATUS resource (i.e. the `preceding_version_uid`) must be specified in the `If-Match` header.

A list of ITEM_TAGs can be associated with the updated EHR_STATUS, in which case a `openehr-item-tag` or `openehr-version-item-tag` request header can be set as well. 
The corresponding response header(s) will return ITEM_TAGs as they were set by the server - see [item tags headers](overview.html#tag/Requests_and_responses/HTTP-headers/openehr-item-tag-and-openehr-version-item-tag) for more details.

The response will contain the updated EHR_STATUS resource when the `Prefer` header has a value of `return=representation`.
data.description_suggestion: Updates the EHR_STATUS associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. The existing latest `version_uid` of the EHR_STATUS resource must be specified in the `If-Match` header to ensure optimistic locking and prevent concurrent modification conflicts. Optionally, ITEM_TAGs can be associated with the updated EHR_STATUS via `openehr-item-tag` or `openehr-version-item-tag` request headers; the server will return these tags in corresponding response headers. Returns the updated EHR_STATUS resource when the `Prefer` header is set to `return=representation`; otherwise returns a 200 OK response without body. This operation creates a new version of the EHR_STATUS.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description lacks context about optimistic locking semantics and doesn't clearly explain the conditional return behavior. Added explanation of If-Match header purpose, clarification about version creation, and explicit description of return value conditions to help AI agents understand the operation's side effects and response behavior.

data.current_description:
Deletes directory FOLDER associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The existing latest `version_uid` of directory FOLDER resource (i.e. the `preceding_version_uid`) must be specified in the `If-Match` header.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes the directory FOLDER resource associated with the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. The existing latest `version_uid` of the directory FOLDER (specified as `preceding_version_uid`) must be provided in the `If-Match` header for optimistic locking. Returns HTTP 204 No Content on successful deletion. This operation removes the entire directory structure and all contained folder references from the EHR.

message: Operation 'contribution_create' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is too generic. Improved description to explain the CONTRIBUTION's purpose in the EHR context, clarify attribute handling, specify HTTP response codes, and reference the request schema for AI agent clarity.

data.current_description:
We will use the relaxed CONTRIBUTION with the following optional attributes:
  - `uid`: when provided, it will be accepted in case is not in-use, otherwise error will be returned
  - `audit.time_committed`: server will always set it
  - `audit.system_id`: when provided, it will be validated
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new CONTRIBUTION resource for the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. The CONTRIBUTION groups multiple version commits within an EHR for audit purposes. Optional attributes include: `uid` (accepted if not already in use, otherwise returns error), `audit.time_committed` (automatically set by server), and `audit.system_id` (validated if provided). Returns the created CONTRIBUTION resource with HTTP 201 Created status and Location header containing the new contribution_uid. The request body must conform to the NewContribution schema.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description uses informal language ('We will use') and lacks clarity on return values and side effects. Enhanced summary to be more specific and action-oriented. Improved description to explain the CONTRIBUTION's purpose in the EHR context, clarify attribute handling, specify HTTP response codes, and reference the request schema for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'ehr_tags_get' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Original summary is too generic and doesn't clarify the scope (EHR-wide vs specific resources). Description is verbose and could be more concise. Enhanced summary to specify ITEM_TAG retrieval scope. Improved description by reorganizing for clarity, explicitly stating the default behavior when no filters are provided, and adding context about the operation's purpose (audit and metadata tracking) to help AI agents understand when to use it.

data.current_description:
Retrieves the list of ITEM_TAG resources associated with any target VERSION or 
VERSIONED_OBJECT within the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. 

This list can be filtered by the given one or more `tag_key`, `tag_value`, `tag_target_path` query parameters. 
In case no such parameter is provided then all ITEM_TAG resources will be retrieved.

This will return an empty list when there is no matching ITEM_TAG associated with any target within given EHR.
  
More than one ITEM_TAG may be associated with a single target, in which case they are uniquely identified 
by their `key` and `target_path` pair attributes.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all ITEM_TAG resources associated with any VERSION or VERSIONED_OBJECT within the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. Results can be filtered using optional query parameters: `tag_key`, `tag_value`, and `tag_target_path`. When no filter parameters are provided, all ITEM_TAG resources for the EHR are returned. Returns an empty list if no matching tags exist. Multiple ITEM_TAG resources may be associated with a single target, uniquely identified by the combination of `key` and `target_path` attributes. This operation enables audit and metadata tracking across all versioned content within an EHR.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic and doesn't clarify the scope (EHR-wide vs specific resources). Description is verbose and could be more concise. Enhanced summary to specify ITEM_TAG retrieval scope. Improved description by reorganizing for clarity, explicitly stating the default behavior when no filters are provided, and adding context about the operation's purpose (audit and metadata tracking) to help AI agents understand when to use it.

message: Operation 'composition_tags_get' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Original summary is too generic and doesn't distinguish this operation from ehr_tags_get. Description is verbose with grammatical issues ('is be used'). Enhanced summary to specify COMPOSITION-level scope. Improved description by clarifying the two uid_based_id formats upfront with clear examples, reorganizing for better readability, fixing grammatical errors, and adding context about the operation's purpose to help AI agents understand when to use it versus the EHR-level tags operation.

data.current_description:
Retrieves the list of all ITEM_TAG resources associated with a given target COMPOSITION version or 
VERSIONED_COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` and owned by EHR identified by `ehr_id`.

The `uid_based_id` can take a form of an OBJECT_VERSION_ID identifier taken from VERSION.uid.value (i.e. a `version_uid`), 
or a form of a HIER_OBJECT_ID identifier taken from VERSIONED_OBJECT.uid.value (i.e. a `versioned_object_uid`).
The former is used to get the tags of a particular (target) version of the COMPOSITION version (e.g. one identified by `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515::openEHRSys.example.com::1`),
whereas the latter (e.g. an identifier like `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515`) is be used to get the tags of the target VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container.

This will return an empty list when there is no ITEM_TAG associated with the given target.
  
More than one ITEM_TAG may be associated with a single target COMPOSITION or VERSIONED_COMPOSITION, in which case they are uniquely identified 
by their `key` and `target_path` pair attributes.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all ITEM_TAG resources associated with a target COMPOSITION version or VERSIONED_COMPOSITION identified by `uid_based_id` within the EHR identified by `ehr_id`. The `uid_based_id` parameter accepts two formats: (1) OBJECT_VERSION_ID (e.g., `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515::openEHRSys.example.com::1`) to retrieve tags for a specific COMPOSITION version, or (2) HIER_OBJECT_ID (e.g., `8849182c-82ad-4088-a07f-48ead4180515`) to retrieve tags for the entire VERSIONED_COMPOSITION container. Returns an empty list if no tags are associated with the target. Multiple ITEM_TAG resources may be associated with a single target, uniquely identified by the combination of `key` and `target_path` attributes. This operation enables metadata and audit tracking at the COMPOSITION level.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic and doesn't distinguish this operation from ehr_tags_get. Description is verbose with grammatical issues ('is be used'). Enhanced summary to specify COMPOSITION-level scope. Improved description by clarifying the two uid_based_id formats upfront with clear examples, reorganizing for better readability, fixing grammatical errors, and adding context about the operation's purpose to help AI agents understand when to use it versus the EHR-level tags operation.
